program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description: Discover secrets of Bay Area wildlife that you can find in our parks and in your own backyard. • Birds of Prey • Urban & Park Wildlife • Olympians of the East Bay • SF Bay Wildlife • Endangered Species • Marine Mammals • And MORE!

program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description: If you can't come to us, one of our naturalists can visit you! Our programs bring live reptiles to you or we can tailor the theme to fit your needs. Reach out for a list of topics. 1 class at a time, up to 3 classes/day.

program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description: Students explore animal basic needs and habitats while learning about creatures of the Bay. Ask for a specific theme such as habitats and adaptation, birds (dependent on season), food webs, volunteer opportunities and more!

program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description: Enjoy discovering the San Francisco Bay and the creatures that call it home. Find out what's going on between the tides, and how you can help. Low tide – mud flats and rocky shore. High tide – nature walk and marine activity.

program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description: Traverse between activity stations with your group for seasonal guided explorations, a nature walk along the shoreline, wildlife crafts, and more! Depending on the class size, this program may include a break for lunch. Minimum of 4 classes.

program_host: Doug Siden Crab Cove Visitor Center
description:
Experience the magic of the forest. Students will rotate through a variety of forest themed activities including a nature walk, nature craft, tree study, and more.

Park Locations: Roberts Recreation Area (Pre-K - 5th grade) or Reinhardt Redwood Regional Park (2nd - 5th grade).  Minimum of 3 classes.
